nodes.label: GitHub Upload Branch and PR Guide
nodes.norm_label: github upload branch and pr guide

nodes.label: Executable Specifications
nodes.rationale: The MVP SDD defines Gherkin user stories as executable specifications and treats them as the source of truth for acceptance and automated tests.

nodes.label: HTMX Partial Update Pattern
nodes.rationale: The MVP UI design prefers server-rendered pages with HTMX partial refreshes for filters, deletes, settings saves, and dashboard fragments instead of a separate SPA.

nodes.label: Soft Delete Data Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Transactions and CSV artifacts are soft-deleted so standard views can hide removed data while preserving auditability and delete-all-data semantics.

nodes.label: CSV Artifact Import Model
nodes.rationale: Bank CSV files are stored as database artifacts and mapped into transactions and balance calculations, with German bank-export semantics and a 10 MB limit.

nodes.label: Category Rule Sankey Model
nodes.rationale: The extension SDD joins category management, rule-based assignment, manual correction, conflict visibility, and Sankey reporting into one categorization workflow.

nodes.label: Default Category Set
nodes.rationale: A user-specific two-level default category taxonomy is proposed to guarantee every transaction has at least a fallback category such as Sonstiges -> Unkategorisiert.

nodes.label: Rule Conflict Warning
nodes.rationale: When more than one rule matches a transaction, the first rule wins but the UI should still surface additional matching rules as a warning for review.

nodes.label: Sankey Flow Model
nodes.rationale: The Sankey report aggregates last-calendar-year money flows as Income -> Category and Expense -> Category, making category assignment data visually explorable.

nodes.label: Release Automation Workflow
nodes.rationale: The repository documents an automated main-branch pipeline combining Build, Dependabot auto-merge, semantic version bumping, and JReleaser publication.

nodes.label: Navigation and Theme Shell
nodes.rationale: The shared layout centralizes authenticated navigation, responsive sidebar collapse, and persisted light/dark theme switching for all main application pages.
